name: March NAC
fieldNote: National-level field. Top Cadet/Junior saber fencers from all regions. Strong A/B rating opportunity for top finishers.
note: Last NAC before Summer Nationals qualification window closes. Critical for NRPS points.

name: April NAC
fieldNote: West coast NAC. Slightly smaller field due to travel. Still strong A/B opportunity. Last national event before Summer Nationals.
note: Long travel from Northeast. Factor in airfare + hotel. Last chance for NRPS points before Nationals.

name: 2026 Summer Nationals & July Challenge
fieldNote: Largest national field. Top fencers in the country. A-rating opportunity for top finishers. Cadet/Junior events in July Challenge portion.
note: Must qualify via NRPS, regional points (65), or top 25% regional standings. Portland is far from NE — plan travel early.

name: October NAC (2026-27 Season)
fieldNote: Season opener. Strong national field. Sets tone for NRPS standings.
note: 2026-27 season opener. Location TBA. Typically announced by June.

name: November NAC (2026-27)
fieldNote: Confirmed Columbus. Drivable from Northeast. Strong field.
note: Drivable from NE — save on airfare. Columbus Convention Center.

name: 2027 Junior Olympics
fieldNote: Junior Olympics field. Qualification required. B/C rating opportunity.
note: Must qualify through regional standings or NRPS. OKC announced Jan 2026.

name: NE Region SYC #1 (est.)
fieldNote: Regional youth circuit. C/D rating opportunity depending on field strength. Regional points toward qualification.
note: SYCs don't require qualification — just age eligibility. Good for regional points accumulation. Cadet events at SYC may count as RCC events.

name: NE Region SYC #2 (est.)
note: Check USA Fencing regional calendar for confirmed dates.

name: NE Region ROC #1 (est.)
fieldNote: Regional Open Circuit. Stronger field than SYC. B/C rating opportunity with strong field.
note: ROCs are open division — Junior/Cadet fencers can enter for rating and experience against older competitors.

name: NE Region RCC/RJCC (est.)
fieldNote: Regional Cadet/Junior Circuit. Targeted age group. Regional points for Cadet and Junior qualification.
note: Key event for regional points accumulation toward JO and Nationals qualification.

name: West-Rock JO Qualifier (NE)
fieldNote: Local qualifier. D/E rating opportunity. Small field but counts for regional points.
note: Fencing Academy of Westchester. Local NE qualifier.

name: NE Section Local Tournament (monthly est.)
fieldNote: Local section tournaments. E/D rating depending on field. Smaller fields but consistent regional point opportunities.
note: Check askFRED for your section's monthly tournaments. Sections 1-4 have active local circuits.

name: NE Region ROC #2 (est.)
note: Good open-division experience and rating opportunity.

name: NE Region SYC #3 (est.)
note: Key regional points opportunity before JO qualification window.
